segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: OK, so what I'd like you to do now is to talk to your partner about your presentations on urban planning.
segments.zh: 好的，现在我想让你们和搭档讨论一下关于城市规划的演示。你们应该已经完成了大部分阅读，所以我想让你们分享想法，讨论演示的结构以及下一步需要做什么。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: You should have done most of the reading now, so I'd like you to share your ideas, and talk about the structure of your presentation and what you need to do next.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: OK Rob.
segments.zh: 好的，罗布。我很高兴我们选了一个相当具体的主题——建在海边的城市。这让查找相关信息容易多了。

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: I'm glad we chose quite a specific topic – cities built next to the sea.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: It made it much easier to find relevant information.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 是啊。而且城市发展得这么快——我是说，我们知道现在世界上一半以上的人口都住在城市里。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: And cities are growing so quickly – I mean, we know that more than half the world's population lives in cities now.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: Yeah, though that's all cities, not just ones on the coast.
segments.zh: 是的，不过那是所有城市，不只是沿海的。但实际上大多数大城市都建在海边，我以前没意识到这一点。

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: But most of the biggest cities are actually built on the sea, I'd not realised that before.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Nor me.
segments.zh: 我也是。而且，很多城市建在河流入海的地方。但显然这可能是个问题。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: And what's more, a lot of them are built at places where rivers come out into the sea.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: But apparently this can be a problem.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: Why?
segments.zh: 为什么？

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Well, as the city expands, agriculture and industry tend to spread further inland along the rivers, and so agriculture moves even further inland up the river.
segments.zh: 嗯，随着城市扩张，农业和工业往往会沿着河流向内陆延伸，所以农业会沿着河流向更远的内陆移动。这不一定是个问题，只是意味着越来越多的污染物被排放到河流中。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: That's not necessarily a problem, except it means more and more pollutants are discharged into the rivers.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: So these are brought downstream to the cities?
segments.zh: 所以这些污染物被带到下游的城市？

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 对。嗯，你读过那篇关于美国东海岸迈阿密的文章吗？

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Hmm.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Did you read that article about Miami, on the east coast of the USA?

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: No.
segments.zh: 没有。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Well, apparently back in the 1950s they built channels to drain away the water in case of flooding.
segments.zh: 嗯，显然在20世纪50年代，他们修建了渠道来排干洪水时的积水。

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: Sounds sensible.
segments.zh: 听起来挺明智的。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Yeah, they spent quite a lot of money on them.
segments.zh: 是啊，他们花了不少钱。但他们没有考虑到全球变暖。他们把排水渠建得太靠近海平面，现在海平面上升，这些渠道几乎没用了。如果下大雨，水排不出去，无处可去。整个设计都有缺陷。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: But what they didn't take into account was global warming.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: So they built the drainage channels too close to sea level, and now sea levels are rising, they're more or less useless.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: If there's a lot of rain, the water can't run away, there's nowhere for it to go.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: The whole design was faulty.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: So what are the authorities doing about it now?
segments.zh: 那现在当局在做什么呢？

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: I don't know.
segments.zh: 我不知道。我确实读到他们计划在未来十年内停止向海洋排放废水。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: I did read that they're aiming to stop disposing of waste water into the ocean over the next ten years.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: But that won't help with flood prevention now, will it?
segments.zh: 但那对现在的防洪没什么帮助，对吧？

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: No. Really they just need to find the money to replace the drainage channels, in order to protect against flooding now.
segments.zh: 不，其实他们现在只需要筹钱更换排水渠，以防洪水。但从长远来看，他们需要考虑整个生态系统。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: But in the long term they need to consider the whole ecosystem.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 没错。不过，沿海城市确实无法独自解决自己的问题，对吧？我的意思是，他们必须开始在国际层面上共同行动，而不是各自为政。

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: Really, though, coastal cities can't deal with their problems on their own, can they?

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: I mean, they've got to start acting together at an international level instead of just doing their own thing.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Absolutely.
segments.zh: 完全同意。问题是，每个人都知道问题所在，环保人士也相当清楚我们应该怎么做，所以他们应该能在一定程度上合作。但各国要就他们准备遵守的原则达成一致，还需要很长时间。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: The thing is, everyone knows what the problems are and environmentalists have a pretty good idea of what we should be doing about them, so they should be able to work together to some extent.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: But it's going to be a long time before countries come to a decision on what principles they're prepared to abide by.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: Yes, if they ever do.
segments.zh: 是啊，如果他们真能达成的话。

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: So I think we've probably got enough for our presentation.
segments.zh: 所以我觉得我们做演示的内容应该够了。只有十五分钟。

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: It's only fifteen minutes.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。那么我想我们首先介绍一些关于沿海城市为何建立的历史背景。但我们不想花太多时间在这上面，其他学生应该已经了解一些了。这主要与交通等因素有关。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: So I suppose we'll begin with some general historical background about why coastal cities were established.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: But we don't want to spend too long on that, the other students will already know a bit about it.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: It's all to do with communications and so on.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。我们应该提到一些地理因素，比如湿地、河口和海岸侵蚀等。我们可以准备一些不同城市的地图，标出这些特征。

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: We should mention some geographical factors, things like wetlands and river estuaries and coastal erosion and so on.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: We could have some maps of different cities with these features marked.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: On a handout you mean?
segments.zh: 你是说发讲义？还是用幻灯片让大家都能看到？

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Or some slides everyone can see?

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: Yeah, that'd be better.
segments.zh: 嗯，那样更好。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: It'd be good to go into past mistakes in a bit more detail.
segments.zh: 最好能更详细地谈谈过去的错误。你读过那个关于几年前新奥尔良洪水问题的案例研究吗？

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Did you read that case study of the problems there were in New Orleans with flooding a few years ago?

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 读过。我们可以用它作为那部分演讲的基础。我觉得其他同学没读过，但他们应该记得当时听说过洪水的事。

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: We could use that as the basis for that part of the talk.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: I don't think the other students will have read it, but they'll remember hearing about the flooding at the time.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好，那背景介绍应该够了。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: So that's probably enough background.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: So then we'll go on to talk about what action's being taken to deal with the problems of coastal cities.
segments.zh: 然后我们接着谈谈正在采取什么行动来应对沿海城市的问题。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好。我们还需要谈什么？也许可以谈谈未来的风险，更长远地看，如果人口继续增长的话。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: What else do we need to talk about?

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Maybe something on future risks, looking more at the long term, if populations continue to grow.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 对。那方面我们得做点功课，我信息不多，你呢？

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: We'll need to do a bit of work there, I haven't got much information, have you?

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: No. We'll need to look at some websites.
segments.zh: 没有。我们得上些网站查查，应该不会花太长时间。

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: Shouldn't take too long.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。我觉得我们最后应该谈谈国际影响。也许我们可以问问观众。我们这里有来自很多不同地方的人。

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: And I think we should end by talking about international implications.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: Maybe we could ask people in the audience.

segments.speaker: CARLA
segments.en: We've got people from quite a lot of different places.

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: That'd be interesting, if we have time, yes.
segments.zh: 那会很有趣，如果我们有时间的话，是的。那么现在我们……

segments.speaker: ROB
segments.en: So now shall we ...

items.prompt: Carla and Rob were surprised to learn that coastal cities
According to Rob, building coastal cities near to rivers

pairs.q: building coastal cities near to rivers
pairs.note: 题干说'在河流附近建沿海城市'，原文用'城市扩张'来指代同一过程，是概括性替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问建沿海城市靠近河流的影响，定位句以'as the city expands'开头，与题干同义，后面内容即为答案所在。

items.prompt: What mistake was made when building water drainage channels in Miami in the 1950s?

pairs.p: what they didn't take into account
pairs.note: 题干问'错误'，原文用'没有考虑'来表述错误的原因。
题干和原文几乎一致，直接对应。
题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'what they didn't take into account'对应题干中的'mistake'，而'built the drainage channels too close to sea level'直接对应题干中的'building water drainage channels'和'too close to sea level'，因此答案C正确。

items.prompt: What do Rob and Carla think that the authorities in Miami should do immediately?

pairs.note: immediately 和 now 都表示立即、马上，是同义替换。
题干说 authorities should do，原文说 they need to，主语和动作进行了同义改写。
原文直接出现 replace the drainage channels，与题干完全一致。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'they just need to find the money to replace the drainage channels, in order to protect against flooding now'，其中 'now' 对应题干的 'immediately'，'they just need to' 对应 'authorities should do'，而 'replace the drainage channels' 是原词复现，因此答案选择 B。

items.prompt: What do they agree should be the priority for international action?

pairs.note: 题干问国际行动的优先事项，原文说环保人士应共同努力，这是对优先事项的转述。
题干中的agree对应原文的should be able to，表示一致意见。

paraphrase.explain: 通过定位句中的'should be able to work together'对应题干中的'agree'和'priority for international action'，从而确定答案A。

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'Historical background'在原文开头直接出现，因此可以快速定位到该句。

pairs.note: 题干说地理因素，原文具体化为'建在海边'，这是地理因素的一种具体表现。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问地理因素，原文提到大多数大城市建在海边，这是地理因素的具体例子，因此定位到选项A。

pairs.note: 题干说过去的错误，原文具体化为洪水事件，属于同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'Past mistakes'对应原文中提到的具体事件'the flooding'，通过上下文理解定位。

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的 'International implications' 在原文中逐字出现，直接定位到该句。
